title: Unable to compile generated C++ file with systemverilog with struct input
body:
I think the problem is related to latest change to support systemverilog struct in verilator. I found it when implementing my RSA verilog project.

# The example to show this issue:

```systemverilog
typedef struct {
    logic [255:0] msg;
} StructType;

module TestMod (
    input logic clk,
    StructType in
);

logic [255:0] msg;

always_ff @( posedge clk ) begin
    msg <= in.msg;
end

endmodule
```

Execute following commands:
```shell
verilator --cc test.sv
make -C obj_dir -f Vtest.mk
```

# The error message:
I share the error message using the [gist link](https://gist.github.com/yodalee/7b2d96216f98f50870bc7577d6aca28c)

# What 'verilator --version' are you using?
I compile this case using the git master commit
Verilator 5.007 devel rev v5.006-101-g47a7e7584

# What OS and distribution are you using?
Archlinux kernel 6.1.8-arch1-1

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Yes I think I can take a look into this. It seems that there are lack some of the implementation to  `VL_TO_STRING_W`
